title: The Bottle Deposit (Part 1)

jokes.text: The concept of being separated is strange. It's like you're engaged to be divorced.
jokes.setup: The strangeness of marital separation
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to being 'engaged to be divorced'
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with clever wordplay. The parallel structure of 'engaged to be married' vs 'engaged to be divorced' is economical and witty.

jokes.text: We think actually some sort of giant rubber band might be the solution.
jokes.setup: Describing the back-and-forth nature of a troubled relationship
jokes.punchline: The absurd solution of a giant rubber band
jokes.explanation: Solid visual absurdism that extends the relationship metaphor logically but unexpectedly.

jokes.text: Then the kid finds out his parents are breaking up... thinks it's because they got caught fooling around, which is probably true.
jokes.setup: Child's innocent misunderstanding of parental separation
jokes.punchline: 'which is probably true' - dark twist on the innocent interpretation
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion that takes an innocent child's perspective and reveals a cynical adult truth.

jokes.text: You look like Brenda Starr. -Is that good? -Better than Dondi.
jokes.setup: Compliment about looking like Brenda Starr
jokes.punchline: Better than Dondi - obscure comic reference that's worse
jokes.explanation: Relies on pop culture knowledge for humor. Economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: I've always thought David was kind of sponge-worthy.
jokes.setup: Discussing attraction to married David
jokes.punchline: Using Elaine's established 'sponge-worthy' criteria
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency callback. 'Sponge-worthy' became a cultural touchstone.

jokes.text: I've been waiting out their marriage for three years. Me too. Well, I've been waiting out two or three marriages... but this is the one I really had my eye on.
jokes.setup: Jerry waiting out one marriage for three years
jokes.punchline: Elaine casually mentioning she's waiting out multiple marriages
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that reveals both characters' predatory dating approach. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That's mine. -You have a car? -My friend lent it to me for the week. -She's out of town. -I've never seen you drive. -Me neither.
jokes.setup: Elaine claiming ownership of poorly parked car
jokes.punchline: She's never seen herself drive either
jokes.explanation: Good character-driven absurdity. The logic of never having seen oneself drive is oddly profound.

jokes.text: A physician married to a salesman. I gotta tell you, Beth, you could have done a lot better than him.
jokes.setup: George learning about the couple's professions
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting the husband to his wife's face
jokes.explanation: Pure George Costanza tactlessness. Sets up major plot point while being perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Hey, I got the body of a taut, preteen Swedish boy.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Kramer's changing body and clothes
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific and inappropriate body comparison
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Kramer absurdity. The specificity ('taut, preteen Swedish boy') makes it memorably bizarre.

jokes.text: Don't think I won't, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Jerry questioning if Kramer can still wear jeans
jokes.punchline: Kramer's defensive threat about buying jeans
jokes.explanation: Kramer's wounded pride over jeans is absurd but believable for his character.

jokes.text: How about a left turn signal, you moron!
jokes.setup: Elaine saying how great it is to drive again
jokes.punchline: Immediately road raging at other drivers
jokes.explanation: Quick character contradiction. The immediate shift from joy to rage is well-executed.

jokes.text: You know what, on my first road test, I hit a dog. I think it was a golden retriever. No, no, no, it was a yellow lab.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she's the worst driver
jokes.punchline: Casually correcting the breed of dog she hit during driving test
jokes.explanation: Dark humor made funnier by the casual correction of details, as if that's the important part.

jokes.text: It's like when a tenant dies in a rent-controlled building. You gotta take immediate action.
jokes.setup: Discussing competition for newly separated couple
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to vulturish apartment hunting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that perfectly captures the predatory nature of their dating strategy. Very New York-specific.

jokes.text: Their grieving time is a luxury I can't afford.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentioning the couple needs grieving time
jokes.punchline: Elaine treating basic human decency as unaffordable luxury
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elaine selfishness. Frames basic empathy as economic constraint.

jokes.text: Calling, calling, calling. -Why are we calling? Why do we call?
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out excuse for calling the separated couple
jokes.punchline: Getting lost in existential questioning about calling itself
jokes.explanation: Shows them overthinking simple social interaction to absurd degree. Slightly overextended.

jokes.text: we slowly remove the two words 'for you'... and we're just there.
jokes.setup: Strategy of calling to say 'I'm there for you'
jokes.punchline: Gradually removing the altruistic part to just be 'there'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deconstruction of fake sympathy. The gradual removal strategy is both clever and cynical.

jokes.text: I mean, I was just being folksy. They could tell I was being folksy? -Yeah, I thought you were being folksy. -Totally folksy.
jokes.setup: George worried his comment wasn't taken as innocent
jokes.punchline: Everyone reassuring him he was 'folksy' in increasingly hollow way
jokes.explanation: George's self-deception and friends' hollow reassurance. The word 'folksy' becomes absurd through repetition.

jokes.text: Kramer, they're painted on. -Well, they're the slim fit. -Slim fit? -Yeah, they're streamlined. You're walking like Frankenstein.
jokes.setup: Kramer bought extremely tight jeans
jokes.punchline: Visual of Kramer walking like Frankenstein in painted-on jeans
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy setup for extended physical gag. Kramer's denial about 'streamlined' jeans adds character layer.

jokes.text: I can't get my pants off. Mickey's audition is in 20 minutes. I'm supposed to be a businessman. I gotta be in costume.
jokes.setup: Kramer's jeans are too tight
jokes.punchline: He's stuck in them and needs them off for an acting role
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy predicament with character-specific stakes (the acting audition).

jokes.text: You're gonna need the Jaws of Life to get out of those things.
jokes.setup: Trying unsuccessfully to get Kramer out of tight jeans
jokes.punchline: Reference to emergency equipment for car crashes
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the physical comedy situation with vivid imagery.

jokes.text: Well, actually, it had a lot to do with George's comment. -Is that right? -I thought maybe I could do better.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking how the separation happened
jokes.punchline: George's tactless comment actually worked
jokes.explanation: Plot development that's also comedically ironic - George's social failure becomes accidental success.

jokes.text: where he asks questions to himself aloud... and then answers them. Am I happy Beth left me? Of course not. Can I hope to pick up the pieces and move on? Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Beth describing David's annoying habit
jokes.punchline: David immediately demonstrating the exact habit she just described
jokes.explanation: Perfect show-don't-tell comedy. The immediate demonstration of the described annoying habit is expertly structured.

jokes.text: Well, David, the thing about George is that... he's an idiot.
jokes.setup: Jerry starting diplomatic explanation of George
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment that George is an idiot
jokes.explanation: Jerry's fake diplomatic buildup followed by brutal honesty. The pause before 'he's an idiot' sells it.

jokes.text: If it hadn't been for that secretary of yours.... I said, sit down. Are you deaf, Bradley? I said, sit down.
jokes.setup: Mickey trying to do dramatic scene with Kramer
jokes.punchline: Kramer physically cannot sit down due to tight jeans
jokes.explanation: Great convergence of storylines. Mickey's frustration builds as Kramer literally cannot follow the scene's requirements.

jokes.text: Sit down, you big, stupid ape!
jokes.setup: Mickey's mounting frustration with Kramer not sitting
jokes.punchline: Final explosion of rage at Kramer's physical inability
jokes.explanation: Climax of the audition disaster. Mickey's final insult caps the escalating frustration.

jokes.text: Did I have a great time with David Lukner last night? I sure did. Do I think there's a future here? I don't see why not.
jokes.setup: Jerry alone, reflecting on his date
jokes.punchline: Jerry adopting David's annoying habit of self-questioning
jokes.explanation: Jerry unconsciously adopting the annoying habit he just heard about shows how infectious bad behavior is.

jokes.text: All right, don't make me get physical here!
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to stop George from sabotaging their plan
jokes.punchline: Elaine threatening violence to protect her dating scheme
jokes.explanation: Elaine's desperation leading to threats shows how important this is to her, but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Articulate? Me? I've never articulated anything. I'm completely incoherent.
jokes.setup: Beth praising George for articulating her feelings perfectly
jokes.punchline: George's coherent denial of being coherent
jokes.explanation: Perfect George self-deprecation that ironically proves Beth's point. The contradiction is beautifully structured.

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing over there? Mind your own business. Now, stay out of my affairs! Jerry, Jerry, that is so sweet of you... but actually, I already ate.
jokes.setup: Jerry angrily calling George at Beth's apartment
jokes.punchline: George pretending it's a sweet social call while Beth listens
jokes.explanation: Brilliant phone conversation misdirection. George's ability to reframe Jerry's anger as sweetness is peak social manipulation.

jokes.text: What is it about the chocolate and the mint... that makes it go so well together?
jokes.setup: George continuing to deflect Jerry's angry phone call
jokes.punchline: Philosophical musing about ice cream flavors as misdirection
jokes.explanation: Extension of the phone gag with George getting more absurdly philosophical to maintain the charade.

jokes.text: My father left us when I was 9... so I guess that's why I have such a fear of abandonment. -Wow, that's so touching. -Yes, it is.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to open up emotionally to Beth
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately agreeing that his own trauma story is touching
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry emotional unavailability. His immediate self-congratulation undercuts any genuine moment.

jokes.text: Oh, fiancée? Boy, you could've done a lot better than him.
jokes.setup: David meeting George's fiancée Susan
jokes.punchline: David using George's exact line against him
jokes.explanation: Perfect karmic comeback. David using George's exact words creates satisfying story symmetry.

jokes.text: It's Frankenstein! It's Frankenstein!
jokes.setup: Child seeing Kramer in his tight jeans
jokes.punchline: Child's terror at Kramer's appearance
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier Frankenstein reference. Child's innocent terror at Kramer's appearance.

jokes.text: He says, 'Boy, you could do a lot better than this guy.' He said the exact same thing that I said to him... just to get back at me.
jokes.setup: George explaining what David said to Susan
jokes.punchline: George recognizing his own words used against him
jokes.explanation: George's awareness of the karmic justice doesn't make it less painful for him.

jokes.text: Do you appreciate this? You see the irony of this? You see what's going on here? What's the matter? I'm nauseous. That what's hurting your appreciation?
jokes.setup: George trying to get Jerry to appreciate the ironic situation
jokes.punchline: Jerry's physical discomfort preventing proper story appreciation
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on storytelling and audience engagement. George frustrated that his perfect ironic story is being underappreciated.

jokes.text: Joey, there you are. Hey, Joey! It's a monster! I'm the babysitter.
jokes.setup: Kramer trying to calm the terrified child
jokes.punchline: Child still seeing him as a monster despite explanation
jokes.explanation: Extension of the physical comedy. Kramer's logical explanation doesn't help his frightening appearance.

jokes.text: I've decided to go with the chicken.
jokes.setup: Susan wanting to talk about the wedding after David's insult
jokes.punchline: Mundane catering decision after relationship crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimatic punchline. After all the relationship drama, Susan's concern is purely logistical.

jokes.text: What are you, deaf? I said, sit down. Hey, for the last time, sit down. -Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Police officer arresting Kramer
jokes.punchline: Same 'sit down' command Kramer couldn't follow in audition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that brings the jeans storyline full circle. The police using Mickey's exact frustrated words.

tmdb_overview: Jerry has an argument with his mechanic; Newman becomes obsessed with Michigan's recycling program.
